title:
POLLINATIONS WEEKLY vol.42 — speedruns & drop-in endpoints → INFRASTRUCTURE SPEEDRUNS
We optimized the Seed tier auto-upgrade workflow
summary:
Previously, calculating GitHub activity to upgrade contributors took about 25 hours to complete. It now takes a few seconds. Turns out you don't actually need to bend the space-time continuum to parse a list of commits. Speaking of time, Pollen credit refills are now hourly instead of daily. If you burn through your local allocation, just go get a coffee and come back. → OPENAI DROP-IN & NEW MODELS
You can now use our image generation endpoints as a drop-in replacement for OpenAI's API. Change the base URL, keep your existing code, get pixels back. We also routed a mountain of new models into the infrastructure:
1. Pruna p-image, p-image-edit, and p-video
2. dirtberry and dirtberry-pro
3. claude-airforce via api.airforce
4. qwen3-tts via our new community provider Seraphyn → BYOP & THE ECOSYSTEM
Bring Your Own Provider (BYOP) auth is becoming the standard. We wired it up for the AI Dungeon Master and Product Packaging apps, alongside a new media upload service. We also fixed a CORS-adjacent blocker that was preventing browser extensions from generating App Keys. Finally, Polly v2 is live with native-speed internals, and the frontend got a cozy redesign with a new scene background and palette system
